operating_state: Revenue declined 11% to $6.96 billion driven by complete elimination of COVID-19 antibody sales ($1.47 billion prior year) and Alimta generic erosion, partially offset by Mounjaro launch generating $568.5 million. Trulicity grew 14% to $1.98 billion despite supply constraints, while Verzenio surged 60% to $750.9 million and Jardiance increased 38% to $577.5 million. Humalog revenue fell 25% to $460.9 million due to pricing pressures and competitive dynamics.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved 3.1 percentage points to 76.6% primarily due to elimination of low-margin COVID-19 antibody sales, partially offset by pricing pressures. Research and development expenses increased 23% driven by higher late-stage asset development costs. Marketing, selling, and administrative expenses rose 12% due to new product launch activities and indication expansions.
material_events: Issued $4 billion in debt across four tranches with maturities from 2026 to 2063 to fund general business purposes and commercial paper repayment. Repurchased $750 million in shares under the $5 billion authorization program. Paid $1.02 billion in dividends at $1.13 per share. Announced March 1, 2023 Humalog price reductions and insulin affordability program expansion.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange rates unfavorably impacted revenue by 2% with continued exposure to euro, yen, and yuan fluctuations. Inflation Reduction Act implementation beginning 2023 creates accelerated revenue erosion risk for selected products starting 2026, with nine-year timeline reducing small molecule innovation attractiveness. Global cost inflation and supply chain strain from geopolitical tensions including Russia-Ukraine war affecting manufacturing and logistics costs.
forward_outlook: Expects tight incretin product supplies to persist until additional manufacturing capacity becomes operational over next several years with significant 2023 expansion. Committed over $8 billion in capital expenditures for new facilities in Indiana, North Carolina, and Ireland. No anticipated COVID-19 antibody revenue in 2023. Continuing Humalog revenue decline expected due to pricing pressures and competitive dynamics.
uncertainty_profile: Donanemab received complete response letter from FDA for accelerated approval pathway with Phase III trials ongoing. Mirikizumab received FDA complete response letter in April 2023 despite positive European regulatory opinion. Multiple Phase II and III trial discontinuations including Rezpegaldesleukin and Solanezumab due to failed endpoints. Ongoing litigation related to 340B program and insulin access matters with potential adverse business impact.
